## Branch 1: `fix/fb-webhook-integration-config`

**Author**: iamnaii@MacBook-Pro-khxng-Akenarin.local  
**Commit**: `72da1b25` — fix(facebook-webhook): resolve verify token + app secret from IntegrationConfig  
**Last updated**: 2026-05-28

### File Changes

```
apps/api/src/modules/chat-adapters/chat-adapters.module.ts        |  3 +
apps/api/src/modules/chat-adapters/facebook-webhook.controller.spec.ts | 68 ++++
apps/api/src/modules/chat-adapters/facebook-webhook.controller.ts  | 36 +++--
3 files changed, 96 insertions(+), 11 deletions(-)
```

### What This Does

Migrates `FacebookWebhookController` from hard-coded environment-variable lookup
(`FB_VERIFY_TOKEN`, `FB_APP_SECRET` via `ConfigService`) to DB-backed
`IntegrationConfigService.getConfig('facebook')` with env as fallback.
This allows the owner to configure the Facebook app secret and verify token
through the Settings → Integrations UI instead of requiring a redeploy.

### Issues

#### Info — `FacebookWebhookController` not documented in `security.md` allow-list

**File**: `apps/api/src/modules/chat-adapters/facebook-webhook.controller.ts`

The controller is intentionally public (no `JwtAuthGuard`) because it is a
Facebook Messenger webhook receiver authenticated via HMAC-SHA256 signature.
The code comment correctly documents this intent. However, `security.md`'s
"Intentionally Public Endpoints" allow-list does not include `facebook-webhook`.

The allow-list currently documents: `chatbot-finance-liff`, `sms-webhook`,
`paysolutions`, `address`, `shop/public-config`.

This is a pre-existing gap, not introduced by this PR. **Suggested follow-up**:
add `facebook-webhook` (and any other pre-existing omissions) to the
`security.md` allow-list so auditors have a complete picture.

### Security Check Results

- `@UseGuards(JwtAuthGuard)` — N/A: intentionally public webhook (HMAC-verified) ✅
- `Number()` on money fields — none found ✅
- `deletedAt: null` in queries — no new Prisma queries added ✅
- Hardcoded secrets — none; secrets moved FROM env TO DB (improvement) ✅
- `@Roles()` decorators — N/A: public controller ✅
- `$queryRaw` unparameterized — none ✅

### Recommendation: ✅ APPROVE

The change is a security improvement (DB-managed secrets → fewer env-var
deploys needed) and is well-tested with 3 new `verifyWebhook` integration
test cases covering the happy path, wrong-token rejection, and
empty-token fail-closed behavior.

## Branch 2: `fix/letters-e2e-sales-assertion`

**Author**: iamnaii@MacBook-Pro-khxng-Akenarin.local  
**Commit**: `8f3439b6` — fix(letters): E2E SALES assertion was matching CANCELLED tab button  
**Last updated**: 2026-05-26

### File Changes

```
apps/web/e2e/letters-page.spec.ts | 12 ++++----
1 file changed, 8 insertions(+), 4 deletions(-)
```

### What This Does

The previous E2E test for the SALES role checked that `button[name='ยกเลิก']`
had count 0 on the letters page. The assertion was incorrectly triggering on
the "CANCELLED" status tab button, which also carries the label "ยกเลิก",
causing false failures.

The fix replaces the brittle button-label assertion with:
1. A page heading visibility check (`จัดการจดหมาย`)
2. A URL check (`/letters`)

This correctly validates that SALES can reach the page (the access-control
question) while delegating the "no Cancel row-button" concern to unit tests
and the API's role guard (`403` for `POST /overdue/letters/:id/cancel` with
SALES role).

### Issues

None.

### Recommendation: ✅ APPROVE

Test-only change. The new assertions are more resilient and correctly separate
access control (E2E) from UI element visibility (unit tests).

## Branch 3: `feat/canned-response-channel-tabs`

**Author**: iamnaii@MacBook-Pro-khxng-Akenarin.local  
**Commits**:
- `d7b6c4bf` — fix(canned-response): Phase 2b — review issues C/W1/W2 channel tabs
- `bc4603d3` — feat(canned-response): add per-channel tabs in template editor  
**Last updated**: 2026-05-25

### File Changes

```
apps/web/src/pages/canned-response-admin/BubbleList.tsx           |  86 +++--
apps/web/src/pages/canned-response-admin/ChannelTabs.tsx          |  63 +++++ (NEW)
apps/web/src/pages/canned-response-admin/TemplateEditorPane.tsx   |  17 ++--
apps/web/src/pages/canned-response-admin/bubble-reorder-logic.test.ts | 100 +++++ (NEW)
apps/web/src/pages/canned-response-admin/bubble-reorder-logic.ts  |  31 +++++ (NEW)
5 files changed, 277 insertions(+), 20 deletions(-)
```

### What This Does

Adds a per-channel tab strip to the canned response template editor, allowing
operators to filter/create bubbles scoped to LINE, Facebook, or all channels.
Key behaviours:

- `ChannelTabs` — new tab component (ALL / LINE_FINANCE / FACEBOOK / TikTok etc.)
  with bubble-count badges that hide when all counts are equal (avoids noise
  when all bubbles are universal).
- `BubbleList` — gains `channelFilter` prop: filters visible bubbles to those
  whose `channels[]` is empty (universal) or contains the active channel.
  The add-bubble mutation scopes new bubbles to the active channel automatically.
- Drag-and-drop reorder now operates on `allBubbles` (not just visible ones),
  preserving cross-channel sort order when only a subset is visible.
- `bubble-reorder-logic.ts` — extracted pure function; 7-case unit test suite
  in `bubble-reorder-logic.test.ts`.

### Issues

#### Warning — `onCountsChange` in `useEffect` deps requires caller stability

**File**: `apps/web/src/pages/canned-response-admin/BubbleList.tsx` (approx. line 88)

```tsx
useEffect(() => {
  if (!onCountsChange) return;
  // ...
  onCountsChange(counts);
}, [allBubbles, onCountsChange]);  // ← onCountsChange in deps
```

The current caller (`TemplateEditorPane`) passes `setBubbleCounts` directly —
a React state setter, which is referentially stable across renders. So no
infinite loop in the current call site. However, if a future caller passes an
inline arrow function `(counts) => doSomething(counts)`, the effect will
re-fire on every render, creating an infinite loop.

**Suggested fix**: Document the stability requirement in the prop JSDoc, or
wrap `onCountsChange` in `useCallback` at the call site (or in the hook's
`useEffect` itself via `useCallback`). Since the current implementation is
correct, this is a **Warning** (future-proofing risk), not a Critical issue.

### Security / Pattern Check Results

- `useQuery` / `useMutation` used — ✅
- `queryClient.invalidateQueries()` after mutations — ✅ (via `invalidate()` helper)
- `api.post()` / `api.get()` used — ✅ (no raw `fetch`)
- Design tokens only (no hardcoded hex/gray) — ✅
- `leading-snug` on Thai text — ✅
- `toast.error()` from sonner — ✅
- `any` usage — 1 pre-existing in `queryFn` (not introduced by this diff) ✅
- `aria-pressed` on tab buttons — ✅ (accessible)

### Recommendation: ✅ APPROVE

Feature is well-implemented with a properly tested pure-function extraction
for the reorder logic. The one Warning is a minor forward-looking concern
that does not affect correctness in the current codebase.
